title: Daily Briefing — Mar 18
headline: Tech leads wobble as sector rotation and volatility expansion test the bulls' conviction.
body: Right, so let's look at what the flows are telling us: SPY hovering at $739.09, grinding above the 20 MA (725.32) but with volatility picking up (VIX at 18.62, now above its 20-day), we’re in a tug-of-war between momentum and defensive posture. Most key indices—SPY, QQQ, DIA—approach stretched levels up against rising volatility, so the chase isn’t rewarded here. As always, patience—react, don't predict.

Sector rotation says it all: Energy (XLE $59.41) is still leading with low risk, but Technology (XLK $174.56) is weakening just as Staples (XLP $85.66), Healthcare (XLV $145.55), and Utilities (XLU $44.05) shift into improving momentum. Watch for opportunistic flows out of stretched tech and into sectors like Staples and Healthcare—classic late-cycle behaviour.

Contrarians: while Fear & Greed at 62.9 isn’t in the danger zone, bullish consensus is creeping up, and Financials (XLF $51.63) have not yet broken support—but keep that as your canary. VIX expansion signals underlying nerves. No rule triggers on sentiment extremes today, but breadth isn’t broadening—this is retail chasing leadership, not a new expansion. If it’s in the press, it’s in the price.
callout_body:
SPY: Support at 725.32 (20 MA), resistance at 739+
XLK: Watch 166.55 (20 MA) for pullback support
XLE: Momentum above 57.75 (20 MA) stays bullish
XLF: Needs to hold 51.65 (20 MA)—if it breaks, risk-off
VIX above 18.04 = volatility expansion, caution
action_items: Don’t chase tech here—wait for mean reversion toward the 20 MA on XLK, NVDA, and AAPL.
Rotate partial exposure toward Staples (XLP $85.66) and Healthcare (XLV $145.55) with improving momentum for new entries.
closing_quote: Less trades, more clarity. If bonds freak out—everything breaks. That’s the tweet.
